description: You are a systems integration engineer. The corporate IT portal needs read-only access to live satellite telemetry, but security rules prohibit giving them direct OpenC3 COSMOS API access. Develop and deploy a standalone Python HTTP microservice at /home/ga/Desktop/telemetry_service.py that acts as a secure middleware bridge. The service must listen on port 8000 and provide a GET /api/inst/status endpoint. This endpoint must retrieve telemetry for INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP1, INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP2, and INST HEALTH_STATUS CMD_ACPT_CNT from the OpenC3 COSMOS JSON-RPC API (http://localhost:2900/openc3-api/api) using the authentication token located at /home/ga/.cosmos_token. The service must return a JSON response with the following schema: {"satellite": "INST", "service_status": "ONLINE", "telemetry": {"temperature_1": <float>, "temperature_2": <float>, "commands_accepted": <int>}, "timestamp": "<ISO 8601 datetime>"}. The microservice must be active and accessible upon completion.
